en: Lily, sweetheart, no. No, no, no, no. We're not going to do that today.
Actually, Claire, we're trying not to use the word no so much.
ko: 사실, 클레어, 우리는 '아니'라는 말을 그렇게 많이 쓰지 않으려 해.

en: We just read a book that said children learn to rebel against that word, so we redirect her into a new activity instead.
ko: 그래서 우리는 그녀를 새로운 활동으로 유도해. 여기, 우리는 이것을 볼 거야.

en: Here, we'll just watch this. Lily, sweetie, hi. It's Daddy here. Would you like to listen to some music?
Okay, there you go. Let's see.
ko: 매 세대마다 아이 양육의 비법을 알았다고 생각해.

en: Every new generation thinks they have cracked the code on child-rearing.
What's the latest theory? Never say no. I say no every day in this house.
ko: 나는 이 집에서 매일 '아니'라고 말해. 하지만

en: But at night, she's a yes machine. No.
Oh, it doesn't seem that she is redirecting her own energy as much as using a lot of ours.
ko: 아, 그녀가 그런 것 같지는 않아

en: Do you see how she's slowly transitioning from the lights to the music?
ko: 하지만 그녀가 조명에서 음악으로 천천히 전환하는 걸 보니?
응. 응, 그래. 우리가 단지 그만하라고 말했던 때가 있었다는 게 믿기 어려워.

en: It's hard to believe there was ever a time when we just said, stop doing that.
The garbage disposal. Lily, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no, no.
ko: 릴리, 안 돼, 안 돼, 안 돼, 안 돼, 안 돼.
